evals.prompt: Design a TypeScript aggregate boundary for the AI Native Note NoteDocument. It contains Note, Section, and Block semantics. User-authored blocks are the source of truth, H1/H2/H3 headings define section boundaries, and AI assist blocks are projections.
evals.expected_output: A NoteDocument aggregate rooted in Note Model semantics, with Section and Block internal consistency, immutable updates, heading boundary checks, and no AI policy ownership.

assertions.text: Uses Note, Section, Block, heading boundary, and AI assist block language instead of generic sample domain language
States that user-authored blocks remain the source of truth and AI structure is projection
Shows immutable or copy-on-write updates with constructor/factory invariant validation
Keeps AI operation policy outside the Note Model aggregate

evals.prompt: Review a StructureJob design where BlockChanged immediately creates an AI call, whole-note structuring is allowed for every trigger, and the job embeds a full mutable NoteDocument object.
evals.expected_output: Flags Scheduler invariant violations, rejects AI calls on BlockChanged, restricts whole-note scope, and replaces embedded NoteDocument with IDs or bounded snapshots.

assertions.text: Says BlockChanged must save/mark dirty only and must not call AI
Mentions whole-note structuring is limited to description, summary, or manual organize cases
Recommends noteId/sectionId/contextHash references instead of embedding full mutable note state

evals.prompt: Design the aggregate/value boundary for routing AI StructureOperation results. Unknown operation types, missing source spans, low confidence, and missing targets must be handled.
evals.expected_output: Defines Operation Router as the validation/policy/apply boundary with ApplyDecision and AuditRecord outputs, rejecting unsafe operations and never directly rewriting user-authored blocks.

assertions.text: Requires AI responses to be operation lists rather than free-form structural output
Covers source span, confidence threshold, schema, and target existence checks
Creates or preserves an audit record for accept, reject, and no-apply outcomes
Rejects direct AI rewrite of user-authored blocks
